topic: Free fall from 5 m: time to impact and final velocity
related_knowledge: Free fall near Earth's surface (g ≈ 9.8 m/s^2)
task_background: A small ball is dropped from rest from a height of 5 meters in a uniform gravitational field near Earth's surface; air resistance is neglected.
task_description: Compute the time to reach the ground and the final velocity upon impact for an object dropped from rest from a height of 5 meters under constant gravitational acceleration g ≈ 9.8 m/s^2, neglecting air resistance.

expected_output.description: Numerical results with units and equations used: time to impact t ≈ 1.01 s, final speed v ≈ 9.90 m/s downward; assumptions stated.

symmetry: Time-translation symmetry (constant g, no explicit time dependence)
conservation_law: Mechanical energy conservation (in absence of air resistance)
constraints.boundary_condition: Ground at y = 0; motion terminates at first contact (y = 0).
constraints.initial_condition: At t = 0: y0 = 5 m, v0 = 0 (dropped from rest), g = 9.8 m/s^2 downward.

sub_tasks.expected_output: t ≈ 1.01 s; final velocity ≈ 9.90 m/s downward; show formulas t = sqrt(2h/g) and v = sqrt(2gh).
sub_tasks.description: Apply constant-acceleration kinematics for free fall with no air resistance: compute the time to impact t = sqrt(2h/g) and the final speed v = sqrt(2gh) using h = 5 m and g = 9.8 m/s^2.
